---
title: "Section 2 Analysis"
author: "Suryadyuti Baral"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)
result_tbl2 <- readRDS("result_tbl2.rds")
```


```{r}
# Step 1: Remove seed column and reshape to long format
result_long <- result_tbl2 %>%
  select(-1) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "condition", values_to = "content")

# Step 2: Identify completely empty lists
result_long_processed <- result_long %>%
  mutate(
    is_empty_list = map_lgl(content, ~ is.null(.x) || !is.list(.x) || length(.x) == 0),
    Metrics = map(content, ~ if (is.list(.x) && "Metrics" %in% names(.x)) .x$Metrics else NULL)
  ) %>%
  select(condition, Metrics, is_empty_list)

# Step 3: Count how many empty lists per condition
empty_counts <- result_long_processed %>%
  filter(is_empty_list) %>%
  count(condition, name = "empty_count")

# Step 4: Unnest non-empty Metrics
metrics_df <- result_long_processed %>%
  filter(!is_empty_list) %>%
  select(-is_empty_list) %>%
  unnest(Metrics)

# Step 5: Rename columns
colnames(metrics_df) <- c(
  "condition", "subject", 
  "bias_fda", "mse_fda", "ise_fda", "ie_fda", 
  "bias_lcs", "mse_lcs", "ise_lcs", "ie_lcs"
)

# Step 6: Summarize metrics by condition
summary_stats <- metrics_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(condition, subject), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  group_by(condition, metric) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = round(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    sd = round(sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

empty_counts
summary_stats
```
